title: Deployment Update Strategies
description:
## Deployment Update Strategies

A production Deployment is being updated. The team requires that **at least 3 pods are always available** during the rollout, and at most **5 pods can exist at any one time** (the Deployment has 4 replicas).

Which `strategy` configuration achieves this?

```yaml
spec:
  replicas: 4
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxUnavailable: ???
      maxSurge: ???
```

options.text: `maxUnavailable: 1`, `maxSurge: 1` — at most 1 pod unavailable (3 always up), at most 5 pods total
`maxUnavailable: 0`, `maxSurge: 0` — ensures zero disruption with no extra pods
`maxUnavailable: 2`, `maxSurge: 2` — allows 2 pods down (2 available) and 6 total pods
`maxUnavailable: 4`, `maxSurge: 1` — replaces all pods at once before adding new ones

explanation: With 4 replicas, `maxUnavailable: 1` means at most 1 pod can be unavailable → minimum 3 pods always running. `maxSurge: 1` means at most 1 extra pod can be created → maximum 5 pods total (4+1). This satisfies both constraints. Option B (`maxUnavailable: 0, maxSurge: 0`) is invalid — at least one of them must be non-zero. Option C allows only 2 available pods (violates the minimum-3 requirement). Option D would take all existing pods offline before any new ones are ready.

hints.title: Understanding maxUnavailable and maxSurge
hints.body: maxUnavailable: how many pods can be unavailable during an update (can be a count or %). maxSurge: how many extra pods above the desired count can exist simultaneously.

hints.title: Check the current strategy on a deployment
hints.body: Use jsonpath to inspect the rolling update config of any existing deployment.
